Check pcb_calculator.py and tp1_service.py for any year-based conditional that caps PRS deduction
Private Pension Administrator Malaysia (PPA) official page: https://www.ppa.my/prs-tax-relief/
No changes to the RM3,000 cap or deduction mechanism — only the expiry year changes
dependencies: Integrate Private Retirement Scheme (PRS) Voluntary Deduction with TP1 RM3,000 Relief

userStories.title: e-PCB Plus Prior Month PCB Amendment — Trigger Corrected CP39 Row on Salary Slip Resubmission
userStories.description: When a submitted Salary Slip is cancelled and reissued (e.g., due to salary error, retroactive bonus, or overtime miscalculation), the PCB amount for that employee in the original month changes. The e-PCB Plus portal supports amendment submissions for prior months, where the employer submits corrected records. Currently, the system has no mechanism to detect that a prior-month salary slip amendment has changed PCB and needs to generate an amendment CP39 batch for re-upload to e-PCB Plus.
acceptanceCriteria: When a previously-submitted Salary Slip is cancelled and a new one is submitted for the same employee and period, a 'PCB Amendment Required' flag is set on the payroll period or employee's PCB ledger
A 'Generate CP39 Amendment File' action on the Payroll Period lists all employees with changed PCB vs. the originally submitted CP39 for that month
The amendment CP39 file contains only changed employee rows with the corrected PCB amount and a period reference (YYYYMM)
An audit log entry is created recording the original PCB amount, amended PCB amount, and the amendment submission date
A unit test verifies that cancelling and resubmitting a salary slip triggers the PCB amendment flag
The amendment file format matches the e-PCB Plus correction submission specification
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus allows employers to submit correction data for prior months via the same CP39 upload with a correction period identifier
The existing CP39 generation produces the full current-month file; amendment generation should be delta-only (changed employees only)
Track 'originally submitted' PCB amounts by saving a snapshot of the CP39 data at submission time to a 'PCB Submission Log' child table or doctype
Amendment use cases: salary revision, bonus reclassification, TP3 carry-forward added after initial submission
Reference: e-PCB Plus full guide at landco.my
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Fix CP39 CSV Format — Add All Mandatory LHDN e-PCB Plus Columns
Add audit log for manual LHDN resubmissions

userStories.title: e-PCB Plus Post-Submission Rejection Record Tracking and Targeted Resubmission Workflow
userStories.description: When an employer submits a CP39 batch to e-PCB Plus (MyTax), the system may reject individual employee rows due to invalid or missing TIN, mismatched NRIC, or incorrect tax category code. LHDN confirmed that incomplete data submissions are not processed and must be corrected and resubmitted. The existing PRD covers pre-submission validation (before upload). This story handles the post-submission response: parsing per-employee rejection codes from the e-PCB Plus API response, surfacing actionable errors to HR, and enabling targeted resubmission of only the failed rows without re-uploading the entire batch.
acceptanceCriteria: After submitting to e-PCB Plus, system parses the API response for per-employee error codes and stores them against the corresponding Salary Slip and CP39 row

An 'e-PCB Plus Submission Errors' panel on the Payroll Period or LHDN Developer Tools page shows: employee name, NRIC, rejection error code, human-readable error description, and a 'Fix & Resubmit' action
Common LHDN e-PCB Plus error codes are mapped to plain-language messages (e.g., 'TIN_NOT_FOUND: Employee TIN not registered in LHDN system — update employee TIN and resubmit')
HR can correct the employee data and trigger resubmission for only the rejected rows (not the full batch)
Resubmission creates a new CP39 amendment record for the affected employees with 'Amendment Reason: Rejection Correction'
HR Manager receives an email within 1 hour of any e-PCB Plus batch containing rejection responses
All rejection records and resubmission attempts are retained in the LHDN Submission audit log
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus API documentation: MyTax developer portal (requires registered employer account)
Store rejection payload in a 'PCB Submission Error Log' DocType linked to CP39 submission record
Resubmission must not create a duplicate CP39 row — update original row status to 'Corrected' and add resubmission reference
Differs from pre-submission validation (US-pre-flight): this story is about handling actual API rejection responses after upload
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Pre-Submission e-PCB Plus Employee TIN and Tax Category Completeness Validation

userStories.title: BIK Motor Vehicle Engine Capacity Prescribed Value Table Lookup for EA Form
userStories.description: LHDN's Public Ruling No. 3/2013 (Benefits-in-Kind) defines a graduated prescribed value table for employer-provided motor vehicles based on engine displacement (cc). Different cc bands have different annual prescribed values, e.g., ≤1,000cc = RM1,200/year; 2,001–2,500cc = RM9,000/year. The existing PRD story 'Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module' addresses general BIK as a broader feature. This story specifically implements the motor vehicle engine capacity lookup table required for accurate EA Form Section D reporting when the formula method cannot be used.
acceptanceCriteria: A configurable 'BIK Motor Vehicle Prescribed Value' DocType stores engine capacity bands (cc range) and corresponding annual prescribed values per LHDN gazette
Employee Benefit record for asset type 'Company Car' or 'Motorcycle' has fields: 'Engine Capacity (cc)', 'Petrol Provided (Y/N)', 'Driver Provided (Y/N)'
Monthly BIK value auto-computes as: (prescribed annual value from table ÷ 12) + (RM1,500/12 if petrol provided) + (RM600/month if driver provided)
EA Form Section D auto-populates the vehicle BIK value with a method indicator: 'Prescribed Value' vs 'Formula Method'
Motorcycle prescribed value follows LHDN's separate motorcycle table (lower values than cars)
A unit test asserts correct BIK monthly value for sample cc bands (e.g., 1,800cc engine should return RM4,500/12 = RM375/month prescribed value)
technicalNotes: LHDN Public Ruling No. 3/2013 (updated): prescribed value method used when vehicle cost to employer is unknown or vehicle is provided without ownership transfer
Annual prescribed values range from RM1,200 (≤1,000cc) to RM50,000+ (>3,000cc) — store full table in DocType
Petrol add-on is a fixed RM1,500/year regardless of actual fuel cost
Formula method = (cost of vehicle × 2/3) × depreciation rate — this is a separate calculation path, not covered here
dependencies: Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module
Rebuild EA Form to Include All Mandatory LHDN Section A/B/C/D Fields

userStories.title: Alert on EPF New Employee Registration 7-Day Statutory Deadline
userStories.description: Employers must register every new employee with EPF within 7 working days from commencement of employment. Failure to comply attracts a fine of up to RM10,000 or imprisonment of up to 3 years. While the PRD covers SOCSO Borang 3 new employee notification, it does not address EPF's shorter 7-working-day registration window. A daily alert system should identify employees whose EPF Member Number has not been recorded within the statutory window and escalate to HR Manager.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType has an EPF Member Number field; if blank after 7 working days from Date of Joining, a desk notification and email alert is triggered to HR Manager
Alerts distinguish between 'Approaching Deadline' (day 5-6) and 'OVERDUE' (day 7+) with increasing urgency
Scheduled daily job scans all active employees with no EPF Member Number and hire date older than 5 working days
Alert email includes employee name, department, hire date, working days elapsed, and a direct link to the KWSP i-Akaun employer portal
Once EPF Member Number is entered on the Employee record, alert auto-resolves and logs the resolution date and user
Unit test confirms 7-day window is computed in working days, excluding Gazetted Public Holidays from the calendar
technicalNotes: Employees Provident Fund Act 1991 — employer must register each new employee within 7 working days from date employment commences
Penalty: fine not exceeding RM10,000 OR imprisonment not exceeding 3 years, or both
Registration via KWSP i-Akaun (employer portal) — employee's NRIC is required to generate EPF member number
Separate from SOCSO Borang 3 (30-day window); EPF has a stricter 7-working-day deadline
Implement as a scheduled Frappe job (daily) with the same notification pattern as other LHDN compliance alerts
dependencies: Implement SOCSO Borang 3 — New Employee Notification to PERKESO
Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings

userStories.title: Capture Concurrent Employment Secondary Income for PCB Aggregation
userStories.description: When an employee holds concurrent employment with two or more employers simultaneously (e.g., a director also employed elsewhere, or intra-group multi-company employment), each employer independently calculates PCB only on its own payroll. This causes systematic under-deduction because the combined income pushes the employee into higher tax brackets. LHDN guidance requires employers to consider declared additional income when computing MTD. The existing TP3 story covers sequential prior-employer income; this story addresses concurrent same-period income from another employer.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee profile has a 'Concurrent Employment' section with fields: secondary employer name, monthly gross income from secondary employer, and monthly PCB deducted by secondary employer
When secondary income is declared, the primary employer's PCB calculator uses the aggregate monthly income (primary + secondary) to determine the applicable tax bracket and correct MTD amount
The additional PCB burden (from aggregation) is deducted from the primary employer's payslip and clearly labelled as 'Additional PCB — Concurrent Income'
System warns if the declared secondary employer PCB appears insufficient for the secondary income (below minimum rate for the income band)
EA Form and CP8D from primary employer reflect primary employer income only; a note indicates that employee has declared concurrent income
Unit test: employee with RM6,000 primary + RM2,000 declared secondary income computes PCB on RM8,000 aggregate monthly salary
technicalNotes: LHDN MTD guidelines — employer must base PCB on total remuneration including other employment income declared by the employee
Section 83(1) ITA 1967 places MTD responsibility on each employer for their own payments; concurrent aggregation requires voluntary employee declaration
Distinct from TP3 (prior employer YTD carry-forward): TP3 is for income earned earlier in the same year by a former employer; concurrent income is simultaneous
Employee declaration of secondary income can be captured via a TP1-style form, or a standalone Concurrent Income Declaration document
Reference: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer/
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration
Implement TP3 Carry-Forward Declaration for New Hires (Prior Employer YTD)

userStories.title: Build Platform SOCSO Deduction Engine Skeleton for Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872)
userStories.description: The Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872) was gazetted December 2025 with commencement date to be set by the Minister. It will require platform providers (ride-hailing, delivery, freelance platforms) to deduct Self-Employment Social Security Scheme contributions from gig worker earnings and remit to PERKESO monthly. Unlike standard employment SOCSO, this is a worker-funded scheme only with no employer share. The system must build the deduction engine now so it can be activated by effective date when commencement is gazetted.
acceptanceCriteria: A new worker category 'Gig / Platform Worker' is available in Employee master with a SEIA (Self-Employment) contribution flag
SOCSO Self-Employment Scheme contribution (worker-funded only, zero employer share) is correctly computed per the SEIA rate schedule
A monthly PERKESO remittance file for gig worker contributions is generated separately from standard employer SOCSO bulk upload
A commencement date guard in LHDN Settings prevents SEIA deductions from activating until the official gazette date is entered
Domestic gig workers (home cleaning, babysitting platforms) can be flagged as excluded per the SEIA exemption schedule
technicalNotes: Self-Employment Social Security Act 789 (SEIA) governs the contribution rate schedule; SOCSO Self-Employment Scheme rates apply
Platform providers remit on behalf of gig workers — different deduction logic from standard employer-employee payroll
Implement with a feature flag (effective_date field) in LHDN/PERKESO Settings so it activates on gazette
PERKESO ASSIST upload format for SEIA may differ from standard employer SOCSO bulk upload — verify before implementation
Gig Workers Act 2025 key features: https://www.rahmatlim.com/perspectives/articles/31531/mykh-key-features-of-s-gig-workers-bill-2025

userStories.title: Implement e-PCBN PCB Non-Deduction Certificate Processing for Self-Assessing Employees
userStories.description: LHDN may issue an e-PCBN (Perakuan Tidak Potongan PCB — PCB Non-Deduction Certificate) to an employee who elects to settle income tax via self-assessment (Form B/BE) rather than through monthly employer PCB deduction. Upon receiving an e-PCBN, the employer must immediately cease PCB deduction for that employee for the specified validity period. At certificate expiry, PCB deduction must automatically resume. Continuing to deduct PCB after receiving an e-PCBN constitutes over-withholding and creates a liability to refund the employee.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee master has an 'e-PCBN Certificate' section with: LHDN reference number, issue date, valid_from, and valid_until
PCB calculation produces RM0 for employees with an active e-PCBN during the validity window
An alert is generated 30 days before e-PCBN expiry to prompt HR to confirm whether to resume PCB or note a renewal
After e-PCBN valid_until date passes, PCB automatically resumes at normal computed rates in the next payroll run
Payslip displays 'PCB Exempted — e-PCBN Ref: [reference]' in place of the PCB deduction line to make the reason explicit
technicalNotes: e-PCBN is issued by LHDN to the employee upon the employee's application — the employer must act on the certificate promptly
Certificate specifies the exemption period (typically one calendar year or the remaining portion of the year)
Store e-PCBN reference number in PCB Change Audit Trail DocType for LHDN inspection readiness
PCB Computation Specification 2025 references the e-PCBN mechanism: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/mdahzjwi/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2025.pdf
LHDN employer responsibilities: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer/
dependencies: Implement PCB Change Audit Trail DocType

userStories.title: Alert on Stale TP1 Relief Declarations When New Assessment Year Begins Without Employee Re-Submission
userStories.description: LHDN requires employees to submit a TP1 form for each assessment year. Relief declarations from a prior year are not automatically carried forward. If an employee has not submitted a new TP1 by 31 January of the new year, the employer should default to computing PCB using only the statutory basic personal relief (RM9,000) and no additional claims. Many employers inadvertently continue applying the prior year's reliefs, creating technically non-compliant PCB calculations that may under-withhold tax.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 declaration record has an Assessment Year field and is year-scoped — a 2025 TP1 cannot be used for 2026 computation
On 1 January each year, the system flags all employees whose active TP1 is from the prior year in a 'Stale TP1 Declarations' report
Payroll run shows a per-employee warning for each stale TP1 before the payroll batch is confirmed
HR can choose: (a) acknowledge and apply prior-year TP1 with an audit warning logged, or (b) default to basic RM9,000 relief only for the employee
Employees can be sent a system-generated reminder to re-submit their TP1 before January payroll confirmation
technicalNotes: Assessment year field on TP1 DocType must be indexed to enable this cross-year check
LHDN technically requires new-year TP1 from January, but tolerates brief carry-forward in practice until the CP39 for January is filed
LHDN employer responsibilities: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer/
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Add Life/Medical Insurance Relief Extension to Children on TP1 Form for YA2026
userStories.description: Budget 2026 expanded two existing income tax reliefs to cover children in addition to self and spouse: (1) Life insurance and takaful premium relief (previously RM3,000 for self and spouse) now includes premiums on policies contracted for the lives of children; and (2) Education and medical insurance premium relief (previously RM4,000 for self and spouse) now also covers children. Both reliefs retain their existing aggregate ceilings. Since these are TP1-declarable reliefs that reduce monthly PCB, the TP1(1/2026) form and the PCB computation engine must be updated to capture the child insurance premium sub-category before January 2026 payroll.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 entry form has a sub-field under Life Insurance/Takaful Relief for 'Child Life/Takaful Premium' with an aggregate ceiling check against the combined RM3,000 limit for self + spouse + children
TP1 entry form has a sub-field under Education & Medical Insurance Relief for 'Child Education/Medical Insurance Premium' with an aggregate ceiling check against the combined RM4,000 limit
PCB computation correctly reduces chargeable income by the declared child insurance premiums up to the respective ceilings
Validation prevents the combined (self + spouse + child) insurance relief from exceeding the statutory cap even if individual sub-entries are below the cap
Historical YA2025 and earlier TP1 records do not show child insurance fields — the sub-fields are gated by assessment year
technicalNotes: Budget 2026 tax measures appendix (official): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/ucapan/tax-measures.pdf
Life insurance/takaful relief: RM3,000 aggregate for self + spouse + children (effective YA2026)
Education and medical insurance relief: RM4,000 aggregate for self + spouse + children (effective YA2026)

This story is a sub-task of 'Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes' but warranted as a separate story due to the relief mechanics requiring aggregate ceiling logic across multiple sub-categories
LHDN official PCB computation spec 2026: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/arvlrzh5/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2026.pdf
dependencies: Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes
Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Gig Workers Act 2025 — Mandatory Service Agreement Compliance Tracker for Platform Providers
userStories.description: The Gig Workers Act 2025 (passed by Parliament, 2025) requires platform providers (contracting entities) to sign written service agreements with each gig worker containing 7 specific mandatory terms: parties to the agreement, period, services provided, obligations, rate and payment method of earnings, and entitled benefits. Payment processing must be linked to a validated service agreement record, and agreements must be maintained for MOHR inspection.
acceptanceCriteria: Gig Worker Service Agreement DocType captures all 7 mandatory terms specified in the Act
Payment processing for a gig worker is blocked until a valid signed service agreement is linked to their record
System flags service agreements that are expired (past end date) and blocks new payment cycles
Compliance report lists all active gig workers with agreement status (valid/expired/missing)
Service agreement records are retained for minimum 7 years for MOHR inspection
Alert is raised 30 days before any service agreement expiry to prompt renewal
technicalNotes: Gig Workers Act 2025 — passed by Malaysian Parliament; exact gazetted date to be confirmed
Contracting entities must also establish internal grievance mechanisms (30-day complaint window for workers)
Platform deactivation of gig workers requires adequate notice and hearing; max 14-day suspension before inquiry
Service agreement requirement applies to all platform providers using gig workers regardless of worker count
dependencies: Build Platform SOCSO Deduction Engine Skeleton for Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872)

userStories.title: Gig Workers Act 2025 — 7-Day Payment Deadline Alert for Informal Gig Engagements Without Payment Schedule
userStories.description: The Gig Workers Act 2025 requires contracting entities to remit payment to gig workers within 7 days of service completion when no formal agreement specifies a payment schedule. This is distinct from Employment Act S.19 7-day rule (which applies to regular employees under contracts of service). Payroll must track per-transaction payment deadlines and alert platform operators before violations occur.
acceptanceCriteria: Gig transactions where no payment schedule is specified in the service agreement are flagged for 7-day payment deadline tracking
Alert is raised to the platform operator 1 day before the 7-day payment deadline lapses per transaction
Overdue gig payments (past 7 days without remittance confirmation) appear in a compliance violation log
Dashboard shows pending gig worker payments grouped by days remaining until 7-day deadline
Payment completion records the actual remittance date against the service completion date
Compliance report exportable for MOHR inspection showing all gig payment timelines and any overdue records
technicalNotes: Gig Workers Act 2025 — payment obligation clause for informal/no-agreement payment schedule engagements
Distinct from Employment Act S.19 7-day rule (which covers employees with contracts of service)
7-day clock starts from the date of service completion or delivery confirmation by platform
Failure to pay within 7 days constitutes a criminal offence under the Gig Workers Act 2025
dependencies: Add Wage Payment Deadline Compliance Alerts (Employment Act S.19 7-Day Rule)
Gig Workers Act 2025 — Mandatory Service Agreement Compliance Tracker for Platform Providers

userStories.title: e-PCB Plus Successful Submission — Store Acknowledgement Reference Number on Salary Slip for LHDN Audit Trail
userStories.description: After a successful monthly PCB data submission via e-PCB Plus on MyTax, LHDN issues a submission acknowledgement reference number. This reference must be stored on the corresponding Salary Slip records to create an LHDN-traceable audit trail. This is distinct from the existing rejection tracking story (which records failures) — this story captures successful submission evidence needed during LHDN audits or PCB reconciliation queries.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful e-PCB Plus submission response stores the acknowledgement/receipt reference number on the submission batch record
Acknowledgement reference is propagated to each Salary Slip included in the submitted batch
Salary Slip form displays e-PCB Plus submission reference as a read-only field in the LHDN/Statutory tab
PCB audit export report includes the e-PCB Plus acknowledgement reference per employee per month
If a month is resubmitted as an amendment, both the original and amendment acknowledgement references are retained (not overwritten)
Salary Slips can be searched by e-PCB Plus acknowledgement reference number for cross-referencing during LHDN queries
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus accessible via MyTax portal (mytax.hasil.gov.my), operational from 3 February 2025
Distinct from MyInvois acknowledgement numbers (e-invoices) and from EIS/EPF submission acknowledgements
Distinct from existing 'e-PCB Plus Post-Submission Rejection Record Tracking' story (this covers successes, that covers rejections)
Submission acknowledgement format to be confirmed from e-PCB Plus API/portal response documentation
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
e-PCB Plus Post-Submission Rejection Record Tracking and Targeted Resubmission Workflow

userStories.title: New Hire Statutory Compliance Checklist with Multi-Agency Deadline Tracker
userStories.description: When a new employee is hired, Malaysian law requires multiple statutory notifications and registrations across four agencies (LHDN, KWSP, PERKESO, and HRD Corp where applicable), each with different deadlines. Currently the system has individual features for each notification, but there is no unified onboarding compliance checklist that tracks all outstanding obligations in a single view with escalation alerts. Employers frequently miss one of the deadlines (e.g., SOCSO within 30 days, EPF within 7 days, CP22 within 30 days) because each is managed in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: On new Employee record creation, a New Hire Compliance Checklist is auto-generated with line items: CP22 submission (30 days), EPF new employee registration (7 days), SOCSO Borang 2/3 (30 days), EIS Form IA (30 days), HRD Corp notification (if applicable)
Each checklist item shows deadline date, current status (pending/submitted/overdue), and a quick-link to the relevant submission tool
Overdue items are highlighted in red on the Employee record and appear in a dedicated Compliance Alerts dashboard
Checklist auto-clears each item when the corresponding submission reference is recorded in the system
Daily digest email/notification is sent to HR Manager listing all employees with overdue new-hire compliance items
Checklist export can be used as a MOHR/statutory audit evidence document showing on-time compliance
technicalNotes: CP22: notify LHDN of new employment within 30 days — via e-CP22 MyTax portal
EPF: register new employee with KWSP within 7 days of employment commencement
SOCSO: register new employee within 30 days — Borang 2 (first-time) or Borang 3 (existing PERKESO member)
EIS: register new employee within 30 days via PERKESO ASSIST portal (Form IA)
HRD Corp: notify within 30 days if employer has 10+ Malaysian employees (compulsory levy payer)
This is an orchestration layer on top of the existing individual submission stories — not replacing them
dependencies: Auto-Submit CP22 via e-CP22 MyTax Within 30 Days of New Employee Hire
Alert on EPF New Employee Registration 7-Day Statutory Deadline
Generate SOCSO Borang 2 for First-Time Employee PERKESO Registration via ASSIST Portal
Auto-Generate EIS Form IA New Employee Registration File for PERKESO ASSIST

userStories.title: Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule
userStories.description: Employers who hire Malaysian citizens with a valid Disability Card (Kad OKU) from the Social Welfare Department are entitled to double deduction on remuneration paid to those employees, capped at RM48,000 per employee per year (applies when monthly remuneration does not exceed RM4,000). The payroll system must record OKU status, track the monthly remuneration cap, compute the eligible double deduction amount, and produce an annual supporting schedule for the employer's corporate income tax Form C submission.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has OKU flag, Kad OKU number, and Kad OKU expiry date fields
Payroll calculation checks that OKU employee's monthly remuneration is ≤RM4,000 for double deduction eligibility
Annual double deduction amount is computed as min(total annual cash remuneration, RM48,000) per OKU employee
Warning raised when Kad OKU expiry date is within 60 days so employer can request renewal
Annual OKU Double Deduction Summary report lists each OKU employee with their total remuneration, eligible amount, and computed double deduction for the year of assessment
Report notes that double deduction extended to YA2030 per Budget 2026 and identifies applicable YA range
technicalNotes: Income Tax Act 1967: Section 34(6)(n) — additional deduction on remuneration paid to disabled employees
Eligible cash remuneration: basic salary, OT, bonus, commission, monthly fixed allowances — excludes non-cash BIK
Double deduction cap: RM48,000 per OKU employee per year of assessment
Monthly salary cap for eligibility: RM4,000/month (employee earning above this does not qualify)
Budget 2026 extended double deduction for hiring OKU employees; also extends to care workers training under KPWKM programmes
Employer records claim in corporate tax Form C — payroll provides the supporting schedule

userStories.title: Calculate and Record EPF Late Contribution Interest Penalty per KWSP Act
userStories.description: Under the Employees Provident Fund Act 1991, employers who remit EPF contributions after the monthly payment deadline (15th of the following month, or last working day if 15th is a holiday) are liable to pay dividends/interest on late contributions at 1% per month or RM5 (whichever is higher) until full payment. The payroll system currently tracks EPF contribution schedules but does not compute or record late payment penalties, which are frequently incurred by SMEs and must be reconciled before the annual EPF audit.
acceptanceCriteria: EPF Contribution Batch record has a Payment Date field capturing the actual remittance date
When Payment Date is after the statutory deadline, system computes penalty: 1% of unpaid amount per month (pro-rated daily) or RM5 minimum
Penalty amount is displayed on the EPF Contribution Batch record and flagged for payment in the next cycle
Monthly EPF reconciliation report shows: due date, actual payment date, days late, penalty amount per batch
System alerts the payroll administrator on the 13th of each month if the current month's EPF batch has not been marked as submitted
Annual late payment penalty summary is generated per year for corporate finance reporting
technicalNotes: EPF Act 1991 Section 45: late contribution penalty at 1% per month or RM5 minimum (whichever is higher)
Payment deadline: 15th of the month following the salary month; adjusted to previous business day if 15th is a weekend/holiday
KWSP may issue a penalty notice (Notice 1A) for persistent late payment; employer may appeal within 14 days
Penalty is calculated on total outstanding amount (employer + employee share) per contribution batch
Distinct from SOCSO late payment penalty (6% per annum, minimum RM5 per month)
dependencies: Generate EPF Borang A monthly contribution schedule

userStories.title: Track EP Category Internship Obligation per 1:3 Internship Policy (TalentCorp/MOHR)
userStories.description: Malaysia's Ministry of Human Resources (MOHR) and TalentCorp mandated that companies employing foreign nationals via Employment Pass (EP) must offer structured paid internships to Malaysian students: 3 placements per EP Cat I, 2 per Cat II, 1 per Cat III. Full enforcement begins 1 January 2026 (pilot extended to March 2026). Payroll must track EP category per expatriate, calculate the total internship quota, and record fulfilled placements (minimum 10 weeks, RM500–RM600/month stipend) to produce the annual evidence package supporting future EP renewal applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record stores Employment Pass category (Cat I / Cat II / Cat III) with EP approval reference number and approval date
Company dashboard calculates total internship slots required = sum(EP Cat I × 3 + EP Cat II × 2 + EP Cat III × 1), capped at 2% of total employee headcount
Intern Placement doctype records: intern name, qualification level, TalentCorp programme reference, start/end date, stipend amount, fulfilment status
System validates intern stipend meets minimum: RM600/month for degree/master/DLKM level, RM500/month for diploma/SKM/certificate level
Compliance summary report shows total quota vs. fulfilled placements, with gap count highlighted in red when unfulfilled quota > 0
Alert triggered 60 days before EP renewal date if internship quota not fully met for the current 12-month period
technicalNotes: Add `employment_pass_category` (Select: Cat I / Cat II / Cat III) and `ep_approval_reference` fields to Employee fixture
New DocType `Internship Placement` linked to Company with fields: intern_name, qualification_level, talentcorp_reference, start_date, end_date, monthly_stipend, status
Dashboard card on LHDN Payroll workspace showing quota compliance ratio
Pilot phase extended to 31 March 2026; mandatory from 1 January 2026
Non-compliance does not cancel existing EPs but adversely impacts future EP renewal assessment

userStories.title: FWCMS Foreign Worker Annual Levy Renewal Expiry Date Tracking and Advance Alert per Employee
userStories.description: Foreign worker levies in Malaysia are paid annually via the FWCMS portal, with the expiry date tied to each individual worker's PLKS/permit period (not a fixed calendar date). The existing 'FWCMS Foreign Worker Levy: 10th-of-Month Payment Deadline Alert' story covers monthly payment monitoring; this story covers the annual renewal cycle per individual worker. Employers with many foreign workers at different renewal dates face compliance risk if individual levy expiry dates are not tracked — an expired levy can result in immediate non-compliance and worker removal orders.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record (foreign worker) stores: `fwcms_levy_payment_date`, `fwcms_levy_expiry_date`, `fwcms_receipt_reference`, `fwcms_levy_sector` (Select: Manufacturing | Construction | Plantation | Agriculture | Services)
Levy sector rate table: Manufacturing/Construction/Services = RM1,850/year; Plantation/Agriculture = RM640/year (configurable for future rate changes)
Alerts sent to HR Admin at 90, 60, and 30 days before each worker's individual levy expiry date
Critical alert escalated at 14 days before expiry if renewal receipt not yet recorded
Non-compliant workers dashboard widget: shows workers with levy expiring in <30 days, <60 days, and already expired
Upon recording renewal, system validates receipt reference is provided and auto-calculates next expiry = payment date + 365 days (or per PLKS duration if different)
technicalNotes: FWCMS levy is annual, not monthly — renewal date is per worker based on PLKS expiry
Manufacturing, Construction, Services: RM1,850/year per worker
Plantation, Agriculture: RM640/year per worker
Multi-tier levy increase of RM300-500 under deliberation — rate table must be configurable
FWCMS portal: https://www.fwcms.gov.my — levy paid online, employer receives electronic receipt
Levy paid by employer — cannot deduct from employee salary

userStories.title: MySIP Structured Internship Stipend Minimum Threshold Validation and Double Tax Deduction Payroll Tagging
userStories.description: Employers participating in TalentCorp's National Structured Internship Programme (MySIP) are entitled to a double corporate tax deduction on internship-related expenses (stipends, training costs) from YA 2017 to YA 2025, with potential extension. Payroll must identify intern employees, validate their monthly stipend meets the MySIP minimum threshold, track the TalentCorp Endorsement Letter reference, and produce a year-end summary of MySIP-qualifying expenses per intern for the corporate tax claim. This is a corporate tax benefit tracking feature, not a statutory payroll deduction.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has flag `is_mysip_intern` (Check) and fields: `mysip_qualification_level` (Select: Master/Degree/DLKM | Diploma/SKM/Certificate), `talentcorp_endorsement_reference` (Data)
On payroll run, if `is_mysip_intern` = true, system validates stipend >= RM600 for degree-level and >= RM500 for diploma-level interns; warning displayed if below threshold
Year-end MySIP Summary Report shows: intern name, qualification level, endorsement reference, total stipend paid, total qualifying expenses for double deduction claim
Report includes total claimable double deduction amount = 2x (total stipend + training costs)
Each qualifying Salary Slip is tagged with `mysip_eligible: true` for audit trail
technicalNotes: Minimum stipends: RM600/month for Master, Degree, DLKM, Professional Certificate; RM500/month for Diploma, DKM, SKM Level 1-3
Double deduction applies to: intern allowances, training materials, accommodation/transport
TalentCorp issues Endorsement Letter based on annual internship placements submitted
MySIP double deduction covered YA2017-YA2025; check Budget 2026 for extension status
Reference: https://www.talentcorp.com.my/mysip-incentives/

userStories.title: Budget 2026 TP1: Increase Children's Learning Disability Diagnostic Relief to RM10,000
userStories.description: Budget 2026 increased the individual income tax relief for diagnostic, early intervention, and rehabilitation treatment expenses for children under 18 with learning disabilities (autism, ADHD, global developmental delay, intellectual disability, Down syndrome, specific learning disabilities) from RM6,000 to RM10,000. The TP1 form ceiling and PCB engine must be updated for YA2026 onward.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1(1/2026) learning disability relief field maximum updated from RM6,000 to RM10,000
PCB computation engine caps this relief at RM10,000 for assessment years >= 2026
EA Form Section A correctly reflects the RM10,000 maximum when generating for YA2026
Historical TP1 records for YA2025 and prior retain the RM6,000 cap with no retrospective change
Validation error message updated: 'Maximum RM10,000 for YA2026 children learning disability relief'
technicalNotes: Update TP1 DocType: `learning_disability_relief_amount` max validation from 6000 to 10000
Add assessment year guard: RM10,000 cap only applies for assessment_year >= 2026
Budget 2026 / Finance Bill 2025, effective YA2026
Eligible conditions: autism, ADHD, global developmental delay (GDD), intellectual disability, Down syndrome, specific learning disabilities (dyslexia, dyspraxia, dyscalculia)
dependencies: Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes

userStories.title: Worker Classification Flag: Contract-of-Service vs Contract-for-Service for PCB/WHT Routing
userStories.description: Employees under a Contract of Service (employment contract) are subject to monthly PCB deduction under Section 107A ITA 1967. Workers engaged under a Contract for Service (independent contractors, freelancers, agents) are instead subject to Withholding Tax under Section 107D ITA 1967 and CP58 reporting — not PCB. Misclassification leads to incorrect statutory deductions, potential penalties, and LHDN audit findings. The system needs an explicit classification flag at the worker record level that automatically routes deductions to the correct statutory scheme.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType has `custom_contract_type` field (Select: 'Contract of Service' [default] | 'Contract for Service')
PCB/MTD deductions on Salary Slip apply only for 'Contract of Service' workers
System raises a warning when a 'Contract for Service' worker is added to a Salary Slip payroll run (incorrect workflow — contractor payments use Supplier/Purchase Invoice)
WHT deduction (Section 107D, 2%) and CP58 reporting trigger for 'Contract for Service' workers
Classification changes are logged in an audit trail with timestamp, changed-by user, and reason
technicalNotes: LHDN Public Ruling 3/2019: guidelines to distinguish employment (contract of service) from self-employment (contract for service)
PCB applies under Section 107A ITA 1967 (employer withholding for employees)
WHT applies under Section 107D ITA 1967 (payer deduction for agents/dealers/distributors)
Complements and gates the existing 'Section 107D ITA — WHT 2% Deduction, Monthly Remittance, and CP58 Integration' story
dependencies: Section 107D ITA — WHT 2% Deduction, Monthly Remittance, and CP58 Integration for Agent/Dealer Payments

userStories.title: Budget 2026 TP1: Expand Vaccination Relief Scope to All NPRA-Approved Vaccines
userStories.description: Budget 2026 expanded the RM1,000 vaccination relief (claimable for self, spouse, and children) from a restricted list of named vaccines to all vaccines registered and approved by the National Pharmaceutical Regulatory Agency (NPRA), Ministry of Health. The TP1 form eligibility description must be updated to remove the previously named vaccine restriction so employees can claim any NPRA-registered vaccine expense.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1(1/2026) vaccination relief field description updated to read 'All vaccines registered and approved by NPRA, Ministry of Health' (removing specific vaccine names)
System applies RM1,000 ceiling based on amount only; no vaccine-type restriction or NPRA lookup required
PCB computation includes vaccination relief up to RM1,000 per assessment year for eligible employees
TP1 audit trail and EA Form reflect the updated description for YA2026 declarations
Historical TP1 records for YA2025 retain old description (no retroactive change to prior-year records)
technicalNotes: Update is description/help text only — the relief ceiling of RM1,000 is unchanged
No external NPRA API lookup required; amount-based validation only
Effective YA2026 per Finance Bill 2025
NPRA: National Pharmaceutical Regulatory Agency, Ministry of Health Malaysia
dependencies: Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes

userStories.title: Gross-Up PCB Calculation for Net Pay Guarantee Employment Agreements
userStories.description: Some employment contracts — especially for expatriates and senior executives — guarantee the employee a specific net pay, with the employer bearing all PCB costs. In these cases, the employer-borne PCB is itself a taxable benefit-in-kind, creating a circular gross-up calculation that requires an iterative formula. The system must implement the gross-up computation to derive the correct gross salary and PCB amount, and reflect the employer-borne PCB as a BIK on the EA Form.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has a boolean field `custom_net_pay_guarantee` indicating the employer bears PCB on the employee's behalf
When enabled, Salary Slip applies iterative gross-up: compute gross such that PCB(gross) + net = gross (converge within 5 iterations, accuracy RM0.10)
Employer-borne PCB amount is shown as a BIK line on the EA Form Section C
Salary Slip notes section shows the gross-up computation breakdown: guaranteed net, computed gross, PCB on gross
PCB is computed on the grossed-up gross salary using the standard MTD formula
Gross-up applies only to base salary; EPF and other deductions computed before gross-up
technicalNotes: Gross-up formula (iterative): Gross_n+1 = Net + PCB(Gross_n); converge when |Gross_n+1 - Gross_n| < 0.10
Use Python Decimal module to avoid floating-point errors in convergence loop
Employer-borne PCB is a BIK per LHDN guidelines — must be included on EA Form as taxable benefit
Common in C-suite contracts and expatriate relocation packages
Complements 'Implement Expatriate Gross-Up Calculator and DTA Country Table' (which focuses on DTA/non-resident; this covers net pay guarantee for any employee)
dependencies: Implement PCB/MTD monthly calculation validation
Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module

userStories.title: Enforce ITA Section 82 7-Year Payroll Tax Record Retention (Separate from MOHR 6-Year)
userStories.description: Section 82 of the Income Tax Act 1967 requires employers to keep all payroll-related tax records (EA forms, CP39 files, Borang E acknowledgements, TP1/TP2/TP3 forms, MTD payment receipts) for 7 years from the end of the assessment year. This is a separate and longer obligation than the Employment Act's 6-year payroll register requirement (already covered). Non-compliance means LHDN can assess tax on a best-judgment basis and impose penalties. The system must enforce a 7-year retention lock on LHDN-related payroll records distinct from the MOHR 6-year lock.
acceptanceCriteria: A `lhdn_retention_expiry_date` field is set on LHDN-related records (EA Form, CP39 run, Borang E, TP1/TP2/TP3) as assessment_year + 7 years
Attempting to delete or archive LHDN records before their `lhdn_retention_expiry_date` raises a blocking validation error citing 'ITA Section 82 — 7-year retention'
A scheduled report runs annually listing LHDN records eligible for deletion (past their 7-year expiry) for HR Manager review
The retention lock applies independently of the Employment Act 6-year lock on payslips — an EA Form for YA2019 (issued Feb 2020) cannot be deleted before Feb 2027
System clearly labels the retention reason as 'ITA Section 82' vs 'Employment Act S.60A' so audit trails distinguish the two obligations
technicalNotes: ITA Section 82: records must be kept 7 years from the end of the year to which any income relates
LHDN Public Ruling No. 5/2000 (revised) defines what constitutes 'sufficient records' including payroll records, EA forms, and MTD receipts
Reference: ANC Group explanation of Section 82 (https://ancgroup.biz/keep-sufficient-records-for-7-years/)
Existing story 'Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention' covers MOHR Employment Act obligation — this story is additive for LHDN-specific records
dependencies: Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention and LHDN/MOHR Audit Export

userStories.title: CP500 Bi-Monthly Advance Tax Installment Awareness Panel for Directors with Non-Salary Income
userStories.description: Directors and sole proprietors who have non-employment income (dividends, rental, business profits) receive LHDN CP500 notices requiring 6 bi-monthly advance tax installments in March, May, July, September, November, and January. ERPNext payroll operates the PCB deduction for salary, but does not track whether the same director also has CP500 obligations outside payroll. HR/payroll admins need visibility into which directors are CP500 payers so they can advise against over-deducting PCB on salary and help directors avoid double-paying or underpaying. LHDN intensified CP500 issuance in 2025-2026.
acceptanceCriteria: A `cp500_payer` boolean flag and `cp500_annual_installment_amount` field can be set on the Director/Employee record
If `cp500_payer = True`, the payroll summary for that director includes an advisory note: 'Employee has CP500 obligations — confirm PCB deduction does not exceed actual tax liability'
A CP500 Installment Tracker child table on the Employee allows recording installment due dates (Mar/May/Jul/Sep/Nov/Jan) and payment reference numbers
Dashboard widget on LHDN Payroll Workspace shows directors with upcoming CP500 installment due dates within next 30 days
System does not auto-deduct CP500 (it is paid directly by the director to LHDN) — it is advisory and tracking only
technicalNotes: CP500 is issued by LHDN to individuals with non-salary income; 6 bi-monthly installments across the calendar year
The payment schedule: installment months are March, May, July, September, November, January
Revision of CP500 amount: first revision by 30 June, second by 31 October of the assessment year
Reference: LHDN CP500 guide (https://www.ajobthing.com/resources/blog/cp500-installment-pay-tax-malaysia)

userStories.title: PDPA 2024 Data Portability and Payroll Employee Data Export on Request
userStories.description: The Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Act 2024 (gazetted and phased in January–June 2025) introduced a new data portability right: employees (data subjects) can request their personal data be transferred from the employer (data controller) to another party in a machine-readable format within a prescribed period. For payroll, this means employees can request an export of all their payroll data (payslips, EA forms, PCB deductions, leave records, statutory contribution history) in a portable format. The existing PDPA story covers DPO appointment and breach notification — this story covers the portability right specifically for payroll records.
acceptanceCriteria: An employee self-service action 'Request My Payroll Data Export' generates a structured export (JSON or CSV) of all the requesting employee's payroll records within the system
The export includes: all payslips, EA forms, TP1/TP2/TP3 records, EPF/SOCSO/EIS contributions per month, PCB deductions, and leave records for the current and prior 7 years
The export is generated within 7 days of request (system setting for response SLA) and is made available for download via a secure, time-limited link
A 'Data Subject Request Log' records who requested data, when, and when/how it was fulfilled — retained for audit
The export format is machine-readable (JSON preferred) and human-readable (PDF summary) — both generated simultaneously
technicalNotes: PDPA Amendment Act 2024 passed 31 July 2024; portability right phased in by June 2025
Portability right applies where processing is by automated means and based on consent or contract
Employment relationship creates a contract basis — payroll data qualifies for portability

Reference: Mayer Brown PDPA analysis (https://www.mayerbrown.com/en/insights/publications/2025/07/from-legislative-reform-to-practical-guidance-key-amendments-to-malaysias-pdpa-and-the-launch-of-cross-border-transfer-guidelines)
dependencies: PDPA 2024 DPO Appointment Registry and 72-Hour Payroll Data Breach Notification Workflow
Build Employee Self-Service Portal for Payslips, EA Forms, and TP1 Submission

userStories.title: EPF i-Akaun Contribution File Whole Ringgit Rounding Rule Enforcement
userStories.description: KWSP mandates that all EPF contribution amounts must be paid and submitted in whole Ringgit Malaysia — no sen (cents) are accepted in the i-Akaun TXT/CSV upload file or during payment. The i-Akaun file generator must apply the correct rounding to both employer and employee contribution values before writing them to the file. Currently the system may generate decimal values causing file validation rejection by the KWSP portal, requiring HR to manually correct and re-upload the file each month.
acceptanceCriteria: EPF i-Akaun CSV/TXT file generator rounds each employee's employer-share and employee-share EPF amounts independently to the nearest whole Ringgit (standard arithmetic: ≥ 0.50 rounds up, < 0.50 truncates)
Voluntary excess contributions (i-Topup) are also rounded to whole Ringgit before file output
A per-payroll-run EPF Rounding Summary report shows sen absorbed or lost per employee line, with a batch total, for auditing
File upload to KWSP i-Akaun succeeds without format validation rejection due to decimal values in any contribution column
Unit test: employee salary RM3,333.33 → employee EPF at 11% = RM366.667 → file output RM367; employer at 13% = RM433.333 → file output RM433
technicalNotes: KWSP official rule: 'Contributions must be paid in Ringgit Malaysia (RM) only, without cents, before the 15th of the following month'
Source: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/employer/responsibilities/mandatory-contribution
Apply rounding at file-generation time only — payroll internal amounts must retain full precision (sens) for payslip accuracy and EPF balance reconciliation
The rounding sen difference is a legitimate cost/credit variance — it is NOT deducted additionally from the employee
Both the TXT (legacy) and CSV (current) i-Akaun formats require whole ringgit contribution columns
dependencies: Generate EPF i-Akaun Electronic Upload File

userStories.title: e-Invoice Mandatory Phase Readiness Check for Employer Company Records
userStories.description: LHDN's e-invoice mandate rolls out in phases based on annual turnover: Phase 1 (>RM100M, Aug 2024), Phase 2 (>RM25M, Jan 2025), Phase 3 (>RM150K–RM500K, July 2025), and Phase 4+ (all remaining businesses). Many employers using the payroll app are SMEs who crossed Phase 3 threshold in July 2025 and must now have MyInvois credentials configured. The payroll system should detect whether the employer's revenue tier requires active e-invoice compliance and warn HR if MyInvois API credentials are missing or the configuration is incomplete.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType includes custom_annual_revenue (Currency) and a read-only custom_einvoice_mandate_phase (Data) field auto-derived from revenue: '>RM100M (Phase 1)', '>RM25M (Phase 2)', '>RM150K (Phase 3)', 'All Businesses (Phase 4+)', or 'Below RM150K (Voluntary)'

On Company save, if einvoice_mandate_phase is not 'Below RM150K' and custom_client_id or custom_client_secret is blank, a prominent warning banner is shown: 'e-Invoice mandatory for this company — MyInvois API credentials are missing'
An e-Invoice Readiness Report lists all Companies in the system, their mandate phase, and compliance status (Compliant / Credentials Missing / Voluntary / Not Applicable)
Alert email is sent to HR Admin when a Company's custom_annual_revenue change moves it into a new mandatory phase
The readiness check is a pre-flight guard only — it does not prevent payroll runs but flags the MyInvois gap for action
technicalNotes: LHDN e-Invoice phase schedule: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/e-invoice/
Phase 3 (from July 2025): taxpayers with annual revenue RM150K–RM500K are now mandatory
Phase 4/5 (all remaining taxpayers with any turnover): LHDN has not finalized the date for the final phase — configure as 'TBD' or rely on LHDN announcement
This check complements the existing myinvois_erpgulf app setup — it validates the payroll employer configuration, not the e-invoice submission flow itself

userStories.title: Progressive Wage Policy Government Incentive Subsidy Claim Tracking Workflow
userStories.description: The Progressive Wage Policy (PWP), expanded in 2025 with RM200 million budget, provides participating employers a government incentive of RM300 per eligible employee per month for up to 12 months. Eligible employees must earn between RM1,500 and RM4,999/month and have at least 12 months' service. Participating companies must provide skill-development training as a condition. The payroll system currently tracks only the PWP eligibility flag but has no workflow for subsidy claim application submission, per-employee entitlement tracking, or alert when the 12-month subsidy claim window nears expiry.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType includes custom_pwp_enrolled (boolean), custom_pwp_enrollment_date, custom_pwp_approval_reference (from MOHR portal), and custom_pwp_subsidy_months_claimed (0–12)
PWP Eligibility Report auto-identifies employees earning RM1,500–RM4,999 with ≥12 months service and flags those not yet enrolled for HR action
Monthly PWP Claim Schedule report generates a summary: enrolled employee count, RM300 × months per employee, cumulative total subsidy entitlement vs claimed to date
An alert fires when a PWP-enrolled employee's salary revision takes them above RM4,999 (exits the subsidy band) or when their 12-month claim window is within 2 months of expiry
Training Evidence checkbox on Employee allows HR to mark that the required skill training evidence has been recorded as a condition of PWP participation
technicalNotes: PWP is administered by MOHR / TalentCorp — employers apply via the MOHR portal and receive an approval reference
Latest expansion: RM200 million budget targeting 50,000 workers, salary increase from RM1,900 to RM2,200 at entry level confirmed as of Dec 2025
Source: Malay Mail report (23 Feb 2026): https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/02/23/pay-rises-on-the-way-progressive-wage-policy-to-boost-salaries-for-over-50000-workers-by-2027/210097
This story extends 'Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report' with the claim-tracking operational workflow
dependencies: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report

userStories.title: MyInvois RM1M Exemption Threshold Update — Revoke Mandatory Flag for Sub-RM1M Companies and Cancel Phase 5
userStories.description: On 6 December 2025, the Malaysian Cabinet approved raising the e-invoice exemption threshold from RM500,000 to RM1,000,000 in annual turnover and cancelled the previously planned Phase 5 rollout (which was due July 2026 for RM500K–RM1M businesses). Any Company records previously configured as Phase 5 mandatory must be reclassified as Voluntarily Exempt to prevent these employers from being incorrectly required to generate self-billed e-invoices for salary slips.
acceptanceCriteria: A one-time data migration sets custom_einvoice_mandatory_phase = 'Exempt' for all companies with custom_annual_revenue_band = '<RM1M'
The migration log captures how many company records were reclassified and notifies the system administrator
LHDN Payroll Compliance Report no longer shows a Phase 5 deadline row for sub-RM1M companies
A notice in the LHDN Payroll Workspace informs users that Phase 5 has been cancelled as of December 2025 and sub-RM1M companies are voluntarily exempt
technicalNotes: Cabinet decision date: 6 December 2025 (confirmed by Comarch, VATupdate, Sovos)
Around 200,000 additional micro and small businesses now fall under this voluntary exemption
Phase 5 previously covered: RM500K–RM1M turnover, mandatory from 1 July 2026 — this phase is now cancelled
Businesses below RM1M may still adopt e-invoicing voluntarily but face no penalties for not doing so
dependencies: MyInvois Phase 4 (January 2026) — Set Mandatory e-Invoice Flag for Companies with RM1M–RM5M Annual Revenue

userStories.title: EIS Amendment 2025 (S.45F) — Employer Job Vacancy Pre-Notification and 7-Day Post-Filling PERKESO Alert
userStories.description: The Employment Insurance System (Amendment) Bill 2025, tabled in Parliament on 4 November 2025, introduces Section 45F requiring employers to notify PERKESO in writing about a job vacancy before hiring and again within 7 days after the position is filled. Non-compliance carries a fine of up to RM10,000 per conviction. A 2-year moratorium will precede enforcement and SMEs are under consultation for a possible exemption, but large employers should begin preparing the workflow now.
acceptanceCriteria: The New Employee onboarding checklist includes a 'Notify PERKESO of filled vacancy (S.45F)' step with due_date = date_of_joining + 7 days
A compliance alert fires if the PERKESO notification step is skipped and more than 7 days have elapsed since the employee's date_of_joining
Company Settings has a flag custom_eis_s45f_applicable (default ON for non-SME; shows a 'SME exemption pending' warning when toggled OFF)
When the flag is ON, the system generates a pre-formatted PERKESO Vacancy Filled Notification letter PDF with employee details, vacancy title, and filling date
technicalNotes: EIS (Amendment) Bill 2025 — Dewan Rakyat first reading 4 November 2025; Section 45F is the new job vacancy notification provision
Two-year moratorium before enforcement penalties apply; SME exemption under stakeholder consultation with PERKESO
Penalty for non-compliance: fine not exceeding RM10,000 on conviction
The Malay Mail (6 Dec 2025) reported that SOCSO plans to exempt SMEs from mandatory vacancy reporting

userStories.title: CP500 Erroneous Issuance Warning — Alert HR When a Pure-Salary Employee Has Received CP500
userStories.description: In December 2025 and January 2026, LHDN issued CP500 (bi-monthly advance tax installment) notices to thousands of salaried employees who had no non-employment income, due to incorrect income classification in their ITRF filings. LHDN confirmed no penalties for 2026 but employees must submit CP502 to cancel or amend the erroneous notice. Payroll systems can assist by detecting employees with 100% employment income (all PCB-deducted) who have been flagged as CP500 recipients, and by generating a corrective CP502 letter template.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType has fields custom_cp500_received (Checkbox) and a computed read-only field custom_cp500_applicable (True only if employee has declared non-employment income on TP1/TP2)

When custom_cp500_received is ticked for an employee where custom_cp500_applicable = False, the system displays a warning: 'This employee appears to have only employment income (PCB deducted). CP500 may be erroneous. Generate CP502 amendment letter for the employee.'

A 'Generate CP502 Amendment Letter' button on the employee record produces a PDF pre-filled with: employee name, income tax reference number, and a declaration that income is solely from employment subject to monthly PCB deduction
A Payroll Report 'CP500 Status Check' lists all employees with cp500_received = True and highlights those where cp500_applicable = False as requiring follow-up
technicalNotes: CP500 is issued to taxpayers with business, rental, royalty, or dividend income; pure-salaried employees should not receive it
LHDN clarification (Dec 2025 / Jan 2026): the CP500 issuance was due to incorrect ITRF income classification by the employee — not an LHDN system error
CP502 form: Application for Amendment of Instalment Payments — deadline for first revision: 30 June; second revision: 31 October
LHDN confirmed no penalties for CP500 non-compliance in 2026 due to the erroneous issuance — source: Malay Mail 5 Jan 2026
dependencies: CP500 Bi-Monthly Advance Tax Installment Awareness Panel for Directors with Non-Salary Income

userStories.title: Employment Act S.60 Rest Day Pay — Enforce RM4,000 Wage Threshold and Tiered Rate Calculation
userStories.description: The Employment (Amendment) Act 2022 (effective 1 January 2023) restricts mandatory rest day pay entitlements to employees earning RM4,000 per month or below. Above this threshold, rest day pay is governed by contract only. The payroll system must enforce this threshold during Salary Slip computation: employees above RM4,000 should not receive statutory rest day pay automatically, and the three-tier rate (0.5x ORP / 1.0x ORP / 2.0x hourly rate) must be correctly applied for eligible employees. Currently the system applies rest day pay without the wage threshold check.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Slip computation applies statutory rest day pay rates (0.5x ORP for ≤half normal hours; 1.0x ORP for >half but ≤normal hours; 2.0x hourly rate for overtime beyond normal hours on rest day) only when employee monthly wage ≤ RM4,000

When employee wage > RM4,000 and a rest day work component is added, the system displays a warning: 'Statutory rest day pay (S.60 EA) does not apply to this employee. Use contractual rate instead.' and blocks the statutory component
Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) divisor is 26 for monthly-rated employees; the computed ORP and resulting rest day component value are shown on the Salary Slip detail panel
A payroll audit report 'Rest Day Pay Compliance Check' lists employees who received rest day pay in the selected period and flags those above RM4,000 monthly salary for HR review
Unit test: employee at RM3,999 receives statutory rest day pay; employee at RM4,001 does not, and a warning is logged
technicalNotes: Employment (Amendment) Act 2022 Section 60(3): overtime/rest day pay provisions excluded for employees above RM4,000/month
Three-tier rest day pay rates per S.60(3): work ≤ half normal hours = 0.5 day ORP; work > half but ≤ normal hours = 1 day ORP; work beyond normal hours = 2x hourly rate
ORP divisor: monthly salary ÷ 26 (Employment Act S.60I) — do not use 30 or calendar days
Manual workers (defined in EA S.2) are entitled to rest day pay regardless of wage — add an is_manual_worker flag on Employee to override the RM4,000 threshold
dependencies: Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator

userStories.title: CP38 Additional Deduction — Include in e-PCB Plus Monthly Upload Alongside CP39
userStories.description: The e-PCB Plus system (launched 3 February 2025) integrates CP39 (monthly PCB) and CP38 (additional tax deduction orders) under a single MyTax employer portal. Previously, CP38 payments were made separately via the ByrHASiL portal. Employers must now ensure that CP38 deduction amounts are included in the monthly e-PCB Plus submission data alongside the normal CP39 PCB figures. The payroll system generates the CP39 upload file but does not currently append CP38 data. Both must be submitted by the 15th of the following month.
acceptanceCriteria: The monthly PCB submission file generator includes CP38 deduction rows for any employee who has an active CP38 Tax Deduction Order with amount > 0 for the payroll period
CP38 and CP39 rows in the upload file are distinguishable by their transaction type code (per LHDN e-PCB Plus file format specification)
The LHDN Payroll Compliance Report shows a combined 'e-PCB Plus Monthly Submission' row with sub-totals for CP39 (normal PCB) and CP38 (additional deductions) separately
A warning alert fires if an employee has a CP38 order active but the payroll module generated no CP38 row in the upload (e.g., zero salary month) — HR must be notified to handle the missed deduction
Unit test: employee with active CP38 order of RM200 and normal PCB of RM500 produces an upload file with two rows — one CP39 row (RM500) and one CP38 row (RM200) for that employee
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus launched 3 February 2025 on MyTax portal — replaces separate e-CP39, e-Data PCB, and ByrHASiL CP38 portals
CP38 = additional tax deduction order issued by LHDN to recover outstanding tax balance via employer payroll deductions
Payment deadline for both CP39 and CP38: 15th of the month following salary payment
LHDN CP38 guide: https://www.hasil.gov.my — search for 'CP38 Tax Deduction Order'; e-PCB Plus file format spec available via e-PCB Plus portal documentation
dependencies: Generate CP39 CSV with Mandatory LHDN e-PCB Plus Columns
CP38 Additional Deduction Fields on Salary Slip

userStories.title: Minimum Wage Enforcement — Penalty Quantum Awareness Panel and Ongoing Violation Daily Fine Tracker
userStories.description: Effective 1 August 2025, the RM1,700 minimum wage applies to all employers including those with fewer than 5 employees. Non-compliance carries fines of up to RM10,000 per employee for a first offence, up to RM1,000 per day for continuing offences after conviction, and up to RM20,000 or 5 years imprisonment for repeat offences. The payroll system's minimum wage validation already blocks non-compliant salary slips, but there is no dashboard showing the penalty exposure if violations are found in an audit (e.g., total underpaid months × RM10,000 per employee).
acceptanceCriteria: The Minimum Wage Compliance Report gains a 'Penalty Exposure Estimate' column: for each employee-month where salary < RM1,700 is detected, the system calculates a worst-case penalty of RM10,000 × number_of_non_compliant_employees and displays the aggregate total

A payroll dashboard widget 'Minimum Wage Compliance' shows: total employees checked this month, count of compliant employees, count of non-compliant employees, and the aggregate penalty exposure estimate
The widget displays three penalty tier descriptions: First offence: RM10,000 per employee; Continuing offence: RM1,000/day post-conviction; Repeat offence: RM20,000 or 5 years imprisonment
An audit history panel shows all payroll periods where minimum wage violations were detected or overridden, with the HR user who approved the override and the stated reason
The system confirms that domestic servants (with custom_is_domestic_servant = True) and apprentices (with custom_is_apprentice = True) are excluded from the minimum wage check and penalty calculation
technicalNotes: Minimum Wages Order 2024 enforcement from 1 August 2025 for all employers (Gazette P.U.(A) 479/2024)
Penalty provisions: National Wages Consultative Council Act 2011 (Act 732), S.36 — fine up to RM10,000 per employee per offence; S.37 — continuing offence up to RM1,000/day
Domestic servants, certain contract apprentices, and commission-only workers (where gross wage including commission ≥ RM1,700) may have special treatment
Enforcement authority: Department of Labour (JTK) — inspections and back-pay recovery orders are common enforcement actions
dependencies: Enforce RM1,700 Minimum Wage for ALL Employers Effective 1 August 2025
Domestic Servant Minimum Wage Exclusion Flag and Alternative Remuneration Tracker

userStories.title: Flexible Work Arrangement (FWA) Payroll Impact Tracker — Adjusted PCB Basis and Payslip Annotation
userStories.description: Under Employment Act S.60P (effective January 2023), employees on approved FWA arrangements may vary their hours, days, or place of work. Where an FWA results in reduced contractual hours or a shift from full-time to part-time (e.g., a 3-day work week), the ordinary rate of pay (ORP), overtime thresholds, and minimum wage compliance calculation must be prorated accordingly. The payroll system approves FWA requests (per the existing S.60P tracker) but does not automatically adjust the ORP divisor, overtime caps, or minimum wage check when an FWA changes the employee's effective working pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: The FWA Record DocType (from existing S.60P story) gains fields: custom_fwa_hours_per_week (Float) and custom_fwa_days_per_week (Integer) to capture the approved reduced work pattern
When an active FWA record exists for an employee, the payroll engine uses custom_fwa_hours_per_week to compute the prorated ORP divisor and overtime threshold for that payroll period

Minimum wage compliance check for FWA employees uses the prorated hourly rate: RM1,700 ÷ 26 ÷ normal daily hours × fwa_daily_hours — a warning fires if the prorated rate falls below RM10.42/hour (RM1,700 ÷ 26 ÷ 8 × 1.6 rounded)
Payslips for employees on FWA include an annotation: 'FWA approved from [start_date] to [end_date]: [N] days/[H] hours per week'
Unit test: full-time employee (5 days, 8h/day) converted to FWA (3 days, 8h/day) has ORP computed on 15.6 days per month (3/5 × 26), not 26
technicalNotes: Employment Act S.60P: employee may apply to vary hours/days/place of work; employer must respond within 60 days — FWA request tracker already built
Prorated ORP for part-time FWA: follow Employment Act S.60I (ORP = monthly salary ÷ 26) but adjust salary to the prorated contracted amount
Minimum hourly rate: RM1,700 minimum wage ÷ 26 working days ÷ 8 hours = RM8.17/hour; FWA employees must still receive at least this per hour worked
PCB for FWA employees: PCB is calculated on actual gross remuneration — no special formula, but ORP-based allowances and rest day pay must reflect the FWA hours
dependencies: Track Flexible Work Arrangement Requests and 60-Day Employer Response

userStories.title: PCB 2026 Spec — Up-Skilling and Self-Enhancement Course Fee Relief Extension to YA2026
userStories.description: Budget 2026 extends the RM2,000 individual tax relief for up-skilling and self-enhancement course fees (claimable by employees) from YA2024 through YA2026. This relief covers HRDC-approved courses and other accredited training. The TP1 Declaration DocType must accept this relief for payroll years 2025 and 2026, and the PCB calculator must correctly exclude it for years where the relief has not been legislated (post-YA2026 unless further extended). A relief validity window must be enforced so the calculator does not incorrectly apply a time-limited relief in a future year.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 Declaration DocType field custom_upskilling_relief (Currency, max RM2,000) is available for tax years YA2024, YA2025, and YA2026 only; for YA2027 and beyond, the field is hidden with a tooltip: 'Up-skilling relief expired at YA2026 — check for gazette extension'
PCB calculator applies the RM2,000 up-skilling deduction only when the payroll year is 2024, 2025, or 2026; a warning appears if an employee has declared the relief for an out-of-scope year
LHDN Payroll Settings DocType has a 'TP1 Relief Validity' child table where each relief type stores: relief_name, max_amount, valid_from_ya, valid_to_ya — up-skilling is seeded as valid YA2024–YA2026
When the valid_to_ya is reached and no extension is detected, the system sends an annual reminder to LHDN Payroll Admin to verify and update the relief table
Unit test: employee declares RM2,000 up-skilling relief in YA2025 → deduction applied; same declaration in YA2027 → blocked with warning
technicalNotes: Budget 2026 source: LHDN PCB 2026 Amendment Spec and Ramco Malaysia Budget 2026 payroll summary at ramco.com
Up-skilling relief scope: courses recognized by Department of Skills Development (DSD) or approved by HRD Corp; maximum RM2,000 per assessment year
This relief is separate from the RM1,000 lifestyle relief — it has its own RM2,000 cap
Gazette reference: confirm via LHDN hasil.gov.my/en/legislation — search 'Income Tax (Relief for Up-Skilling Course) Order'
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration
Generate LHDN PCB 2026 Specification Annual Update Checklist and Version Flag

userStories.title: Employment Pass Succession Plan Compliance Tracker for EP Categories II and III
userStories.description: Effective 1 June 2026, all new and renewal Employment Pass applications for Category II (RM10,000–RM19,999/month, max 10 years) and Category III (RM5,000–RM9,999/month, max 5 years) must include a structured succession plan demonstrating how the expatriate's role will be transferred to a local employee within the approved tenure. Immigration will reject renewal applications without an approved succession plan. Employers with multiple EP holders in these categories face a significant compliance gap if no structured tracking exists in their HR system to manage plan submission status, knowledge transfer milestones, and local successor assignments.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains a custom_ep_succession_plan_status Select field ('Not Required' / 'Draft' / 'Submitted to IMI' / 'Approved' / 'Overdue') visible only when visa_type is 'Employment Pass' and ep_category in ('Category II', 'Category III')

Succession plan fields capture: local_successor_employee (Link to Employee), knowledge_transfer_start_date, knowledge_transfer_target_date, succession_plan_document (Attach), ep_succession_plan_submission_date, ep_succession_plan_imi_reference
An automated alert fires to the HR Manager 90 days before EP renewal date if custom_ep_succession_plan_status is not 'Approved'
A Succession Plan Compliance Report lists all Cat II/III EP holders with their plan status, local successor, and knowledge transfer timeline completion percentage
On EP renewal date with status not 'Approved', the system creates a Compliance Alert DocType record with severity 'Critical' and routes it to the Employer Representative
technicalNotes: Revised EP policy effective 1 June 2026 — Cabinet approval 17 October 2025; announced via IMI portal
EP Category II: RM10,000–RM19,999/month, max 10 years; Category III: RM5,000–RM9,999/month, max 5 years
Succession plan must include: local successor ID, role transfer breakdown, monthly training schedule (24–36 months), assessment milestones, contingency protocols, executive sign-off
Link to existing Compliance Alert or custom lhdn_payroll_integration compliance framework
IMI official announcement: https://esd.imi.gov.my/portal/latest-news/announcement/announcement-266-ep-salary-policy-2026/
dependencies: Employment Pass Minimum Salary Threshold Validator (June 2026 Revised EP Policy)

userStories.title: EA Form and Form E Cross-Reconciliation Pre-Submission Audit Validator
userStories.description: LHDN's 2026 compliance enforcement explicitly uses automated system-based cross-checking: payroll records must reconcile with individual EA Forms, and EA Form aggregate totals must reconcile with Form E (CP8D). Any divergence — omitted BIK values, incorrectly classified allowances, part-timer EA Forms not generated, or PCB amounts differing from payroll — is automatically flagged by LHDN and can trigger an audit without prior warning. Employers currently have no built-in pre-submission reconciliation check in ERPNext. Before finalizing and distributing EA Forms (28 February deadline) and submitting Form E (31 March/30 April), a cross-reconciliation report should flag data consistency failures.
acceptanceCriteria: An EA Form / Form E Reconciliation Check tool runs after all EA Forms are generated for the year and before Form E submission
Check 1 — Payroll-to-EA reconciliation: sum of all Salary Slip gross_pay for the year per employee matches EA Form B1 (gross salary); variance > RM1 is flagged as a Red error
Check 2 — PCB reconciliation: sum of all Salary Slip pcb_deducted for the year matches EA Form D1 (PCB deducted); variance > RM1 is flagged
Check 3 — BIK completeness: if any active employee has a custom_bik_vehicle or custom_bik_housing field populated, their EA Form must include a non-zero BIK value; missing BIK on EA is flagged
Check 4 — EA Form coverage: all employees (including resigned, part-time, and deceased) who received any salary during the year have an EA Form generated; missing EA Forms are listed
Check 5 — Form E aggregate: sum of all EA Form gross_salary values matches the Form E total remuneration field; sum of EA Form PCB values matches Form E total PCB field
A reconciliation summary report with pass/fail per check, total divergence amounts, and drill-down to the offending employee records is generated
The EA Form bulk-email action is blocked if any Red-status reconciliation failures exist, with a confirmation override for Payroll Manager role
technicalNotes: LHDN cross-check methodology: Payroll records → EA Form → Form E must reconcile; automated system detection — no manual review required on LHDN's side
Common audit triggers: omitted BIK (company car, phone), incorrect exempt allowance classification, part-timer/resigned employee EA Forms not issued
PandaHR audit trigger guide: https://pandahrms.com/tax-update-2026-form-e-form-ea-malaysia-employer-compliance-guide-and-common-mistakes-that-trigger-lhdn-audits/
KTP.com.my Form E submission 2026 guide: https://www.ktp.com.my/blog/form-e-submission-2026/29jan2026
Tolerance threshold for rounding differences should be RM1 (not zero) to account for Salary Slip rounding across multiple runs
dependencies: Generate EA Form (Borang EA) for employees
Generate Borang E (Form E) employer annual return
Rebuild EA Form to Include All Mandatory LHDN Section A/B/C/D Fields
Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module

userStories.title: HRD Corp Training Claim Focus Area Category Tagging for Industry 4.0, Green Technology, and Smart Economy (2026)
userStories.description: From 2026, HRD Corp officially expanded its approved training focus areas to explicitly include Industry 4.0, Green Technology and Renewable Energy, and Smart Economy disciplines. Training claims under the SBL-KHAS grant (and related schemes) submitted under these focus areas may qualify for enhanced or prioritised claim processing. Employers who do not tag their training claims with the correct HRD Corp focus area cannot demonstrate alignment with national training objectives, which affects their eligibility for targeted government matching grants and may impact Latihan MADANI levy utilization credit.
