The existing 'HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard' story does not capture the focus area dimension.
acceptanceCriteria: The HRD Corp Training Claim DocType gains a custom_hrdcorp_focus_area Select field with options: 'Industry 4.0', 'Green Technology & Renewable Energy', 'Smart Economy', 'Digital Skills', 'Soft Skills / Leadership', 'Technical / Vocational', 'Other' (matching HRD Corp official category taxonomy)
Training claims with a defined focus area display the category tag on the Levy Utilization Dashboard
The Levy Utilization Dashboard allows filtering by focus area to show: total training hours, total levy claimed, and total employees trained per focus area
A Focus Area Coverage Report shows which HRD Corp categories the employer has utilized in the current and prior two years — useful for demonstrating breadth of training investment
When creating a training claim, the focus area field is mandatory if the Grant Type is 'SBL-KHAS' or 'SBL'
technicalNotes: HRD Corp 2026 digital learning focus areas officially claimable: Industry 4.0, Green Technology, Smart Economy — confirmed in HRD Corp eTRIS portal guidance
SBL-KHAS e-learning daily rate: RM875; in-person soft skills: RM10,500 per day — these rates do not change by focus area but focus area tags are needed for grant pre-approval forms
HRD Corp eTRIS portal: https://hrdcorp.gov.my/
BrioHR HRD Corp overview: https://briohr.com/blog/foreign-worker-levy/
disprz.ai digital learning HRD Corp 2026: https://disprz.ai/blog/hrdf-digital-learning-lms-malaysia
dependencies: HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard
Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty

userStories.title: e-Duti Setem Per-Employee Contract Stamp Duty Compliance Task with 30-Day Countdown (Effective January 2026)
userStories.description: From January 1, 2026, all employment contracts must be stamped within 30 days of signing via LHDN's new e-Duti Setem system (accessible through the MyTax Portal). A fixed stamp duty of RM10 applies per employment contract under Item 4, First Schedule of the Stamp Act 1949. Late stamping incurs escalating penalties. Contracts executed before January 1, 2025 are exempt. The existing story 'Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)' establishes the general framework but does not specify per-employee task auto-creation with countdown. This story implements the operational per-hire task generation and reference number recording.
acceptanceCriteria: On new Employee record creation with a hire_date of 2026-01-01 or later, system auto-creates a 'Contract Stamp Duty' compliance task assigned to HR Manager with due date = hire_date + 25 days (5-day buffer before the 30-day statutory deadline)
Task includes: employee name, hire_date (= contract signing date assumed), stamp duty amount (RM10), and a link to e-Duti Setem on MyTax portal
Employee record gains two fields: custom_stamp_duty_status Select ('Pending' / 'Completed' / 'Overdue') and custom_stamp_duty_ref (Data) for storing the LHDN e-Duti Setem reference number
When custom_stamp_duty_ref is populated, custom_stamp_duty_status auto-updates to 'Completed' and the compliance task is auto-closed
If task is not closed by due date, custom_stamp_duty_status changes to 'Overdue' and an escalation alert fires to Payroll Manager
Employees hired before 2026-01-01 are excluded from stamp duty task creation; a filter is applied on the hire_date
technicalNotes: Stamp Act 1949, First Schedule Item 4: RM10 fixed duty per employment contract
e-Duti Setem portal accessible via MyTax from December 15, 2025; mandatory stamping enforcement from January 1, 2026
30-day deadline runs from the date the contract is executed (signed), which is typically the hire_date or offer acceptance date
For contracts signed before January 1, 2026 but during 2025: no late penalty if stamped by December 31, 2025
LHDN e-Duti Setem announcement: https://stamps.hasil.gov.my/stamps/
The Vibes mandatory stamping announcement: https://www.thevibes.com/articles/news/109052/lhdn-to-enforce-mandatory-stamping-of-employment-contracts-from-2026
dependencies: Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)
New Hire Statutory Compliance Checklist with Multi-Agency Deadline Tracker

userStories.title: Budget 2026 — Double Tax Deduction for Hiring Prisoners Released on Licence and Drug Dependents (YA2026–YA2030)
userStories.description: Malaysia's Budget 2026 expanded the existing further deduction (double deduction) incentive for employers who hire vulnerable persons to include two new categories effective from YA2026 to YA2030: (1) prisoners released on licence under the Prisons Act 1995, and (2) drug/substance dependents and misusers currently undergoing approved treatment or rehabilitation programmes. Previously, the double deduction applied only to OKU (persons with disabilities), senior citizens, single parents, and ex-convicts under the Income Tax Act. The payroll/HR system must flag employees under these new categories, collect the required supporting documentation references, and include them in the annual double deduction schedule submitted as supporting evidence for the employer's tax return.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains two new custom fields: custom_prisoner_licence_status (Check, 'Prisoner Released on Licence under Prisons Act 1995') and custom_drug_rehab_participant (Check, 'Drug/Substance Dependent in Approved Rehabilitation')
Each flag field has a corresponding document reference field: custom_prisoner_licence_doc_ref (Data) and custom_drug_rehab_doc_ref (Data) for storing the approval or certification reference number
YA eligibility guard: flags can only be set for employees hired from YA2026 onward; a validation warning fires if set for employees with hire_date before 2026-01-01

The annual Double Deduction Schedule report (generated before employer tax filing) includes employees in all eligible categories: OKU + Senior Citizen + Single Parent + Ex-Convict + Prisoner on Licence + Drug Rehab Participant
Report groups employees by category with total annual remuneration paid — this is the double deduction base amount for the employer's income tax claim
A warning is triggered if either new flag is set without a corresponding document reference being populated
technicalNotes: Income Tax Act 1967 Schedule 4B (further deductions for employment of vulnerable persons); Budget 2026 gazette expected early 2026
Supporting documents: letter from Prisons Department (for Prisoners Released on Licence); letter from Ministry of Health / Welfare Department (for drug rehabilitation participants)
The deduction is on the employer's corporate tax return, not on the employee's PCB — this is an employer-side tax benefit, not an employee relief
Existing OKU double deduction tracking story: 'Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule' — extend the same module
Budget 2026 key highlights: https://www.crowe.com/my/-/media/crowe/firms/asia-pacific/my/crowemy/news/key-highlights-of-malaysias-budget-2026--for-individuals-and-businesses-newsletter.pdf
dependencies: Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule

userStories.title: Progressive Wage Policy — 21-Hour Annual Training Completion Tracking per Eligible Employee
userStories.description: Employers participating in Malaysia's Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) must ensure each eligible employee (local, full-time, earning RM1,700–RM4,999/month) completes a minimum of 21 training hours per calendar year as a condition for receiving the government wage subsidy (RM200 million budget, targeting 50,000 workers in 2025–2026). Training hours not completed in 2025 can be carried forward until December 2026. The system must track training hours per employee, link to HRD Corp eTRIS-approved training records, and validate the 21-hour threshold for each eligible employee before the PWP subsidy claim is generated. The existing 'Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report' story tracks wage levels but does not address the mandatory training hours dimension.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains a custom_pwp_eligible Check field (auto-set when nationality='Malaysian', employment_type='Full-Time', and basic_salary between RM1,700 and RM4,999)
A PWP Training Log child table (or linked DocType) records training sessions per employee: date, course_name, provider, hours_completed, hrdcorp_etris_ref, and carry_forward_year flag
System computes per-employee: training_hours_this_year (sum of current-year logs) and carry_forward_hours (from prior year, valid until December 2026)

PWP Training Compliance Report lists all eligible employees with: total hours completed, carry-forward hours applied, shortfall vs. 21-hour target, and PWP subsidy claim readiness (Ready / Shortfall / Not Started)
Alert fires to HR Manager by October 31 each year for employees who have not yet met the 21-hour threshold, giving 2 months to schedule remaining training
PWP subsidy claim generation is blocked for employees with training shortfall; override requires Payroll Manager approval
technicalNotes: PWP launched June 2024; RM200 million budget for 2025; targets local private-sector employees in RM1,700–RM4,999 wage band
21-hour training requirement per employee per year; 2025 uncompleted hours carry forward to December 2026
Training providers must be HRD Corp or JTK-approved; government-linked companies and MNCs are excluded from the subsidy scheme
PWP managed by SOCSO; subsidy application via SOCSO portal — not through LHDN
Omni HR PWP guide: https://www.omnihr.co/blog/progressive-wage-model-malaysia
Malaysia Progressive Wage Policy ISIS analysis: https://www.isis.org.my/2024/03/25/malaysias-progressive-wage-policy/
dependencies: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report
HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard

userStories.title: EIS Amendment 2025 S.45F — Moratorium Phase Suppression and Voluntary Job Vacancy Reporting Module
userStories.description: The Employment Insurance System (Amendment) Act 2025 introduces Section 45F requiring employers to notify PERKESO in writing about job vacancies before hiring and confirm fill within 7 days of placement (penalty: RM10,000). However, PERKESO has announced a 2-year moratorium comprising (1) a design-and-consult phase and (2) a voluntary low-risk reporting phase before mandatory enforcement. The existing story 'EIS Amendment 2025 (S.45F) — Employer Job Vacancy Pre-Notification and 7-Day Post-Filling PERKESO Alert' covers the notification mechanism but not the moratorium management. During the moratorium, penalty alerts must be suppressed; employers who choose to voluntarily participate need an opt-in mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: Company record gains a custom_eis_s45f_mode field: 'Moratorium — Not Reporting' / 'Voluntary Reporting (Early Adopter)' / 'Mandatory Reporting' with an effective_date for mode transitions
All EIS S.45F penalty alerts and notifications are suppressed when mode is 'Moratorium — Not Reporting'
When mode is 'Voluntary Reporting', vacancy notification records can be created and submitted; the system logs voluntary submissions separately for early-adopter audit trail
A system-wide compliance reminder fires 90 days before the moratorium end date (configured in System Settings as custom_eis_moratorium_end_date)
On moratorium expiry, custom_eis_s45f_mode auto-transitions to 'Mandatory Reporting' and S.45F notifications become active
Employer groups back EIS vacancy reporting reforms — moratorium phase shows in compliance calendar as 'Voluntary — No Penalty Until [date]'
technicalNotes: EIS (Amendment) Act 2025, new Section 45F — enacted 2025; penalty RM10,000 for non-compliance after enforcement
PERKESO moratorium: 2 years from Act commencement date — Phase 1: design/consult roundtables; Phase 2: voluntary use
SMEs are the primary target for the moratorium extension (Lexology, NST articles)
The reporting mechanism is to PERKESO ASSIST Portal; API endpoint not yet published
Lexology EIS amendment detail: https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=307fc1fa-bdbb-47aa-8a39-89fe02c562ce
NST employer groups backing reforms: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/business/corporate/2025/12/1334538/employer-groups-back-eis-vacancy-reporting-reforms-urge-practical-implementation
dependencies: EIS Amendment 2025 (S.45F) — Employer Job Vacancy Pre-Notification and 7-Day Post-Filling PERKESO Alert
Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings

userStories.title: PTPTN Payroll Deduction Facilitation and Monthly Remittance Tracking Module
userStories.description: Employers who facilitate PTPTN (National Higher Education Fund) student loan repayments via salary deduction must obtain written employee consent under the PTPTN Act 1997 and Section 24 of the Employment Act before processing the deduction. The deducted amount must be remitted monthly to PTPTN separately from statutory deductions, and must be excluded from the PCB computation base and excluded from EA Form employment income columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee PTPTN consent record (written authorisation date, reference number, repayment schedule) stored on Employee DocType
Monthly PTPTN deduction is processed as a non-statutory deduction in Salary Slip — separate from EPF/SOCSO/EIS/PCB
PTPTN deduction amount is excluded from Gross Taxable Salary when computing PCB/MTD
Payslip clearly labels PTPTN deduction in a dedicated 'Other Authorised Deductions' section
Monthly PTPTN remittance schedule generated (employee IC, PTPTN reference number, deduction amount) for HR upload to MyPTPN portal
EA Form Part B does NOT include PTPTN deduction as an income reduction — it is purely a payroll facilitation pass-through
technicalNotes: PTPTN Act 1997 Section 22 authorises salary deduction with borrower consent — employer is a facilitator only, not a statutory collector
PTPTN repayment is NOT a tax-deductible relief for the employee (unlike EPF or life insurance); exclude from TP1 relief fields
Remittance file format: MyPTPN employer portal at https://myptptn.ptptn.gov.my/
Employment Act Section 24(2)(d) allows authorised deductions; combined total of all non-statutory deductions must not reduce net pay below RM1,700 statutory minimum wage

userStories.title: Statutory Contribution Compliance Certificate Bundle Export for Government Procurement Tender
userStories.description: Malaysian government and GLC procurement regulations require suppliers to provide proof of EPF, SOCSO, EIS, and LHDN PCB compliance before bid qualification. Procurement officers request recent contribution payment records and submission histories. The payroll module should auto-generate a formatted compliance evidence bundle covering the last 3–6 months of all statutory submissions, exportable as a structured PDF for tender submission without manual HR compilation.
acceptanceCriteria: Export covers EPF, SOCSO, EIS, and PCB for a configurable date range (last 3 or 6 months), with submission dates, payment references, and total amounts per month
Bundle header displays: company name, employer registration numbers (EPF No., SOCSO No., EIS No., PCB Employer No.), and the date range covered
PDF export formatted for government procurement submission: clear table per statutory body with monthly submission date, amount paid, and payment reference
Data sourced from existing submission tracking records already stored by the payroll module (no re-entry required)
Export generates an index page summarising all statutory bodies, total contributions, and last submission date
Report is accessible from the LHDN Payroll workspace as a one-click export
technicalNotes: Legal basis: Arahan Perbendaharaan (Treasury Instructions) and UKAS procurement guidelines for government suppliers in Malaysia
This report aggregates data from: CP39 remittance records, EPF Borang A records, SOCSO Borang 8A records, EIS submission records
Actual statutory body compliance letters (from KWSP, PERKESO, LHDN) must still be obtained directly from those bodies — this export provides the supporting payroll evidence
EPF compliance portal: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/employer, SOCSO: https://assist.perkeso.gov.my/, LHDN: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/
dependencies: Generate CP39 monthly PCB remittance file
Generate EPF Borang A monthly contribution schedule
Generate SOCSO Borang 8A monthly contribution schedule

userStories.title: Progressive Wage Policy Monthly Payroll Evidence Package Assembly for SOCSO/JTK Compliance Audit
userStories.description: Employers participating in Malaysia's Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) receive a government wage subsidy (up to RM200–RM300/employee/month for 12 months) conditional on meeting occupation-based wage floors and providing at least 21 annual training hours per employee. Monthly payroll evidence (wage level records + training hour logs) must be maintained and are auditable by SOCSO and the Ministry of Human Resources. The payroll system should auto-assemble this evidence package monthly without requiring HR to compile records manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Monthly report lists all PWP-enrolled employees with: current monthly gross wage, minimum required wage per occupation code, compliance status (met/breach), and training hours accumulated YTD
Government subsidy received per employee per month recorded on Employee record and reconciled against SOCSO disbursement
Training hours tracked at employee level with date, hours, training provider, and HRD Corp grant reference — cumulative YTD total visible
Alert raised in Q4 when an enrolled employee has accumulated fewer than 21 annual training hours (with hours deficit shown)
Monthly evidence package export (PDF/Excel) contains: employee wage list, training log, and SOCSO subsidy receipts — formatted per SOCSO/JTK audit requirements
If SOCSO suspends subsidy for wage floor breach, alert HR with specific employee, reason, and corrective action required
technicalNotes: PWP launched January 2025; SOCSO administers the subsidy for eligible employees earning RM1,700–RM4,999/month in covered industries
Government subsidy: up to RM200/month (entry-level) or RM300/month (higher positions) for 12 months per enrolled employee
21-hour annual training must use HRD Corp-registered providers or TVET institutions — link with HRD Corp eTRIS training records
SOCSO as administrator: https://www.perkeso.gov.my/; employers must register participation via SOCSO

This story extends existing stories: 'Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag' (eligibility) and 'Progressive Wage Policy — 21-Hour Annual Training Completion Tracking' (annual tracking) — this adds monthly evidence assembly for audit
Reference: https://www.businesstoday.com.my/2026/02/23/over-51000-workers-benefit-from-progressive-wage-policy/
dependencies: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report
Progressive Wage Policy — 21-Hour Annual Training Completion Tracking per Eligible Employee
Progressive Wage Policy Government Incentive Subsidy Claim Tracking Workflow

userStories.title: SOCSO/EIS RM6,000 Ceiling: Post-Grace Period Retroactive Contribution Correction Audit Tool
userStories.description: The SOCSO and EIS wage ceiling increased from RM5,000 to RM6,000 effective October 1, 2024, with a six-month grace period that expired on March 31, 2025. After the grace period, full compliance is mandatory with penalties applying. Employers who failed to update contributions for employees earning RM5,001–RM6,000 during the grace window may have underpaid. An audit tool to identify affected payroll periods and generate correction schedules is needed — distinct from the existing enforcement story which covers ongoing forward compliance only.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit report identifies all salary slips from October 2024 to March 2025 where employee gross wages were between RM5,001 and RM6,000 and SOCSO/EIS contributions were calculated on the old RM5,000 ceiling
Report quantifies the contribution shortfall per employee per month (employer portion and employee portion separately for SOCSO and EIS)
Report generates a suggested correction schedule showing additional contributions required to bring each affected period to compliance
Summary view shows total employer liability across all affected employees and periods
Corrected contribution amounts are exportable in PERKESO e-Caruman format for submission via PERKESO ASSIST portal
technicalNotes: Old ceiling: RM5,000; new ceiling: RM6,000; transition window: October 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025
Grace period confirmed: Crowe Malaysia advisory states no penalties during Oct 2024 – Mar 2025 transition; full compliance required from April 1, 2025
Contribution table lookup must compare salary slips against both old and new Jadual Kadar Caruman tables
PERKESO ASSIST portal accepts corrected submission via e-Caruman batch upload
dependencies: Enforce SOCSO/EIS Wage Ceiling Update to RM6,000 (October 2024 Amendment)

userStories.title: TP1(1/2026): Update Child Care Centre Relief Age Eligibility Validator from 6 to 12 Years
userStories.description: Budget 2026 amended TP1 to extend child care centre relief eligibility to children up to 12 years old (previously capped at 6 years), covering registered daily care and after-school transit centres. The existing story 'Budget 2026 TP1: Permanent RM3,000 Childcare Relief for JKM-Registered Transit Centres' focuses on JKM-registered centre classification, but does not update the core age eligibility validation rule from ≤6 to ≤12 in the PCB calculation engine. This is a distinct validator change in the MTD calculator.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 child care relief validator accepts child ages 1–12 years; children aged 7–12 previously rejected are now accepted
RM3,000 relief cap is maintained regardless of how many children in the 1–12 age range are declared
PCB recalculation triggers correctly when an employee adds a child aged 7–12 to their TP1 declaration
Existing children aged ≤6 on prior TP1 records are unaffected; new age upper bound of 12 applies from YA2026 onward
technicalNotes: Update age validation rule in TP1 child care section: child.age <= 12 (was child.age <= 6)
Applies to licensed/registered daily care centres and after-school transit centres; registration type validation unchanged
Source form: FORM PCB TP1 (1/2026); effective from YA2026
dependencies: Budget 2026 TP1: Permanent RM3,000 Childcare Relief for JKM-Registered Transit Centres

userStories.title: Foreign-Sourced Income Remittance PCB Exemption Treatment for Malaysian Tax Residents (Effective to YA2026)
userStories.description: Under current exemption orders, certain foreign-sourced income received by Malaysian tax residents and remitted into Malaysia is exempt from income tax (investment income exempt until December 31, 2036; other categories until December 31, 2026). For expatriates and Malaysian employees with offshore income, employers need to distinguish foreign-sourced exempt remittances from Malaysian employment income when computing PCB, and correctly exclude qualifying amounts from the PCB taxable base. This is distinct from the existing DTA 183-day tracker which handles treaty-based exemptions for short-term visiting employees.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record allows flagging of 'Foreign-Sourced Income Exemption' with: source country, exemption basis (Remittance Exemption Order / DTA Article), exempt amount declared for the year, and expiry YA
PCB calculator excludes declared foreign-sourced exempt remittances from annual taxable income when computing monthly MTD for residents
System validates that exemption is only applied to tax residents (≥182 days in Malaysia) and rejects for non-residents
HR records the exemption order reference number for LHDN audit trail
Warning fires if exemption is claimed for income types not covered by the remittance exemption orders (e.g., active business income from foreign operations)
technicalNotes: Exemption under ITA 1967 via P.U.(A) exemption orders: certain FSI remitted by residents exempt until Dec 31, 2026 (investment income until Dec 31, 2036)
Distinct from DTA-based exemption (existing story covers 183-day rule); this covers statutory FSI remittance exemption order
Not applicable to income from employment performed in Malaysia — only to offshore/investment income remitted to Malaysia
PWC Malaysia tax summary 2025 confirms transitional FSI exemption rules in force
dependencies: Implement DTA 183-Day Residency Tracker and Short-Term Assignee PCB Exemption Workflow
Implement Expatriate Gross-Up Calculator and DTA Country Table
